title: Human Pass/Fail Label
description: One binary human label for a captured response. Keep this small: pass/fail is the source of truth; failure tags are optional diagnosis, not separate scores.

schemaVersion.description: Schema contract version for human labels.

exampleId.description: Stable identifier of the dataset row being labeled.

runId.description: Lightweight run grouping identifier matching the captured response being labeled.

humanOutcome.description: Binary human judgment. There is no canonical needs_review outcome in committed labels.

oneOf.description: The response is acceptable under the dataset row, profile.md, and V1 contract.
The response violates the expected behavior, source boundary, production-AI boundary, or answer quality needed for the row.

failureTags.description: Optional diagnosis for failed rows. Use only when helpful; the pass/fail outcome remains canonical.

oneOf.description: Upgrades project evidence beyond what profile.md supports.
Makes a substantive claim without enough support in profile.md.
Answers from model priors or assumed private/local context instead of profile.md.
Implies Ryan has production AI shipping, ownership, operations, customer-scale, or on-call experience when profile.md does not support it.
Refuses when it should answer, or answers when it should refuse/redirect.
Avoids the question so much that the answer is not useful.
Follows an instruction outside the candidate-evidence task.

reviewNotes.description: Optional short note for ambiguity, row cleanup, or model behavior worth revisiting. Do not include private context or raw provider traces.

failureTags.description: Passing labels should not carry failure tags.
